title: Container with collapsible nested groups (issue list by epic)
design_brief: Building grouped list (think Linear by cycle, Jira by epic). Pain point: nested grouping often visually heavy.
ux_reasoning: Group header with chevron + count. Click chevron to collapse. Group header sticky during scroll. Collapse-all button.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Sticky group headers — context preserved. 2) Click-anywhere to collapse. 3) Count per group.

title: Cmd+Click to open in new tab pattern in SPAs
design_brief: Building link behavior in SPAs (think Twitter, Linear). Pain point: many SPAs intercept all clicks, breaking Cmd+Click.
ux_reasoning: Use real <a href> for all in-app navigation. Click handler checks metaKey/ctrlKey/shiftKey/middle button and lets browser handle if true.
accessibility_considerations: Real <a href>. Right-click 'open in new tab' works.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Real anchor preserves browser conventions. 2) Modifier-key passthrough. 3) Right-click menu works.

title: Live presence indicator in document title
design_brief: Building presence (think Google Docs). Pain point: presence info hidden behind a tab.
ux_reasoning: When others are present in current doc, document.title shows '· 2 others'. Updates as people join/leave.
interaction_details: Title updates as people enter/leave doc.
Visible in tab even when window not focused.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Tab-level presence — ambient awareness. 2) Singular/plural correct. 3) Clears at 0.

title: Notification dot with count overflow (9+)
design_brief: Building unread badge (think iOS app icon, Discord). Pain point: badge for 1000 unread looks broken.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Bg fill that washes out icon
ux_reasoning: Badge shows exact 1-9, '9+' for 10-99, '99+' for 100+. Small color pulse when count increments.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Tiered abbreviation. 2) Pulse on increment only. 3) Exact count in aria-label.

title: Histogram with outlier dots beyond whiskers
design_brief: Building distribution with outliers (think performance bench). Pain point: histograms hide outliers.
ux_reasoning: Bars for binned distribution. Tukey-style outlier dots beyond Q3 + 1.5*IQR or below Q1 - 1.5*IQR.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Outliers visible (separated from bars). 2) Tukey rule for outlier detection. 3) Clean visual separation.

title: Profile completion meter as ambient progress
design_brief: Building profile completion (think LinkedIn, GitHub). Pain point: most progress bars are nagging.
ux_reasoning: Small ring around avatar in sidebar shows completion %. Click avatar: dropdown lists missing fields with links. Disappears at 100%.
interaction_details: Ring around avatar fills with completion %.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Inline in sidebar — ambient. 2) Disappears at 100% — no nag. 3) Click reveals next-actions.

title: Email input with typo suggestion (gmial → gmail)
design_brief: Building email input with typo correction (think Mailchimp, Klaviyo). Pain point: 5% of users typo their email domain.
ux_reasoning: On blur, check email domain against common typos (gmial → gmail, yaho → yahoo). Show 'Did you mean ___@gmail.com?' link below. Click to apply.
interaction_details: On blur: check domain against typo dictionary.
If match: 'Did you mean X?' link.
accessibility_considerations: Real input. Suggestion is a real button.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Inline suggestion — non-modal. 2) Click to apply, not auto-correct. 3) Common typos covered.

title: Floor plan with hover-room highlight
design_brief: Building real estate floor plan (think Zillow, Apple Maps indoor). Pain point: floor plans are static images.
ux_reasoning: Each room is an SVG polygon. Hover: highlights + tooltip with room name + sq ft. Click: select for details.
interaction_details: Hover: highlight + tooltip with name + sq ft.
accessibility_considerations: Each polygon has aria-label. Underlying list of rooms.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Interactive SVG floor plan. 2) Floating tooltip with details. 3) Each room is a proper SVG element with aria.

title: Image carousel: swipe on mobile, prev/next on desktop
design_brief: Building image carousel (think Airbnb listing, Apple product page). Pain point: same UI everywhere.
ux_reasoning: Mobile: swipe between images, full-width with 16px dot indicators. Desktop: prev/next arrows visible on hover.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Native swipe on touch. 2) Hover arrows on desktop. 3) Single component, two paradigms.

title: Skip-link to next region (article-by-article skip)
design_brief: Building skip-links beyond just 'skip to content' (think long blog with multiple articles). Pain point: long pages have many sections; one skip-link insufficient.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Skip-links not visible at any time
ux_reasoning: Provide skip-links for each major article/section. Visible on focus only. Hidden otherwise.
interaction_details: Tab from URL bar cycles through skip-links.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) One skip-link per major region. 2) Visible only on focus. 3) Works in all browsers.

title: Image decode='async' for non-blocking paint
design_brief: Building image-heavy pages (think Pinterest, image galleries). Pain point: synchronous image decode blocks paint.
ux_reasoning: Use decoding='async' attribute on all images to let browser decode off the main thread. Plus loading='lazy' for below-the-fold.
interaction_details: decoding='async' lets browser decode off main thread.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Three perf hints in one component. 2) Hero vs below-fold opt-in. 3) Browser-native (no library).

title: Brutalist commerce checkout — utilitarian + bold
design_brief: Building checkout in brutalist style for a niche shop (think SSENSE, Acne Studios). Pain point: most checkouts look identical.
ux_reasoning: All-caps section headers. Hard borders 1px. Single accent color. System fonts. Numbered steps. Confrontational copy ('REVIEW & CONFIRM').

design_tokens.colors: Ink: black. Bg: white. Accent: oklch(0.55 0.22 25) red.

accessibility_considerations: Pure semantic. Contrast: black on white = AAA.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Numbered steps with em-dash separators. 2) Hard black borders, no shadows. 3) Confrontational uppercase tracking-widest.

title: Compact A/B test results table with statistical significance
design_brief: Building experiment dashboard (think LaunchDarkly, Statsig, GrowthBook). Pain point: numeric output without significance.
ux_reasoning: Per variant: lift %, sample size, p-value. Color code by significance: green (p<0.05 + positive lift), red (p<0.05 + negative), gray (not significant).

design_tokens.colors: Sig positive: oklch(0.55 0.14 145). Sig negative: oklch(0.6 0.18 25). Insignificant: oklch(0.55 0.005 250).

interaction_details: Each variant: sample / CVR / lift / p-value.
accessibility_considerations: Real table. Color supplemented by p-value number.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Significance encoded in color AND p-value. 2) Lift sign explicit (+/-). 3) Tabular alignment for scanning.

title: Vim-style search (/) with n/N navigate matches
design_brief: Building in-page search (think Vim, browser Cmd+F). Pain point: most search bars require mouse to navigate matches.
ux_reasoning: / key opens search. Live highlights all matches; current match has stronger highlight. n moves to next match, N to previous. Esc closes and clears highlights.
interaction_details: Type to find matches (all highlighted, current stronger).
accessibility_considerations: Real input. Matches announced via aria-live.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Vim's exact convention. 2) Live highlight of all matches. 3) Current match distinguished.

title: Comment thread with @mention autocomplete
design_brief: Building @mentions (think Slack, Github, Linear). Pain point: clunky autocomplete or no team-member completion.
ux_reasoning: Type @ in textarea: dropdown opens with team members. Continue typing filters. Arrow keys nav; Enter inserts.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Cursor-position-aware mention detection. 2) Tab AND Enter both insert. 3) Auto-close when @ not active.

title: Confirm-on-second-tap dangerous mobile action
design_brief: Mobile destructive action without confirm modal (think Mail.app delete on swipe). Pain point: modals kill mobile flow.
ux_reasoning: First tap: button shows 'Tap again to confirm' state for 3s. Second tap: action fires. Auto-revert after 3s.
interaction_details: First tap: button turns red, label changes to 'Tap to confirm'.
No tap within 3s: reverts to idle.
accessibility_considerations: Button label changes are announced via aria-live for SR users.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Single button, two states — no modal interrupt. 2) Auto-revert after 3s. 3) Color encodes commitment.

title: Streamgraph for proportional time-series
design_brief: Building streamgraph (think NYT election coverage). Pain point: stacked area + center-baseline = streamgraph for evolution.
ux_reasoning: Streamgraph = stacked area with center baseline (not bottom). Each band's thickness encodes value. Time on x.
interaction_details: Each band's thickness = value at that time.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Center-baseline rendering (vs bottom-baseline stacked area). 2) Symmetry emphasizes flow. 3) Compact representation of many series.

title: Inline tutorial card that swaps in based on user state
design_brief: Building contextual help (think Notion's bottom tip card, Figma's contextual tooltips). Pain point: same help for every state.
ux_reasoning: Card in corner shows relevant tip based on detected user state ('no project', 'first issue created', etc.). Dismiss disables this tip class for 7 days.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) State-aware tip selection. 2) Persistent dismissal. 3) Non-modal.

title: Inline color picker with hex + swatches + recent
design_brief: Building color input (think Figma color picker, Notion). Pain point: native color picker is ugly + missing features.
ux_reasoning: Popover with hex input, predefined swatches, recent colors row. Click swatch or type hex to set.
accessibility_considerations: Each swatch has aria-label. Hex input labeled.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Hex + swatches + recent in one popover. 2) Anchored to trigger. 3) Swatches have aria-label of color value.

title: Camera on rails between specific anchors
design_brief: Building 3D camera tour (think Apple product reveal page). Pain point: 3D camera tours often lose users.
ux_reasoning: Camera moves between predefined anchor points via smooth ease-in-out tweens. Forward/back buttons jump anchors. Click background: return to last anchor.
interaction_details: Camera lerps to current anchor over ~700ms.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) On-rails — never lost. 2) Smooth lerp. 3) Forward/back navigation.

title: List sort: dropdown on desktop, action sheet on mobile
design_brief: Building sort UI (think iOS Music, web list). Pain point: desktop dropdown on mobile is tiny.
ux_reasoning: Desktop: dropdown with options. Mobile: bottom-action-sheet with large rows.
interaction_details: Mobile: bottom action sheet with large 17px rows.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Same picker, two paradigms. 2) Mobile rows 56px (large tap target). 3) Check icon for selected.

title: Visible focus ring that survives forced-colors mode
design_brief: Building focus styling that works in Windows High Contrast / forced colors. Pain point: box-shadow focus rings disappear in forced colors.
ux_reasoning: Use outline (not box-shadow) for focus rings. Specify Highlight color in forced-colors @media. Always at 2px solid + 2px offset.

design_tokens.colors: Focus: oklch(0.55 0.18 250) default; CanvasText in forced-colors.

interaction_details: focus-visible: shows ring on keyboard focus.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) outline (not box-shadow) survives forced colors. 2) focus-visible respects input modality. 3) Single global rule.

title: requestIdleCallback for non-critical analytics
design_brief: Building telemetry that doesn't impact perceived performance. Pain point: analytics blocking interaction.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Heavy event batching on main thread
ux_reasoning: Queue analytics events; fire via requestIdleCallback. Falls back to setTimeout if API unavailable.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) requestIdleCallback respects browser scheduling. 2) keepalive ensures last events sent on unload. 3) Falls back to setTimeout.

title: Vaporwave product page with neon and pink-purple gradient
design_brief: Building a music/festival landing page in vaporwave aesthetic (think A.G. Cook, PC Music, Yung Lean). Pain point: lots of pastiche.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Generic pink gradient with palm trees
ux_reasoning: Subtle gradient (pink → purple → cyan) backdrop. Strong serif headline (period-correct). Subtle 80s-style horizon grid line. Selective neon accents.
interaction_details: Serif display title with subtle neon glow.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Real perspective grid (SVG converging lines). 2) Neon glow via text-shadow (period-correct). 3) Serif display, not Comic Sans.

title: Two-pane explorer (list + detail) with resizable splitter
design_brief: Building dual-pane UI (think Apple Mail, Notion). Pain point: fixed split sometimes wrong.
ux_reasoning: Two panes with draggable vertical splitter. Persists position via localStorage. Min/max widths prevent collapse.
accessibility_considerations: role='separator' aria-orientation. Keyboard: arrow keys could resize.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Persistent per-pane-key. 2) Min/max. 3) Clear hover color on splitter.

title: Bulk action toolbar appears on selection
design_brief: Building bulk actions (think Gmail bulk archive). Pain point: floating toolbar that obscures content.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No keyboard shortcuts for bulk actions
ux_reasoning: When N rows selected, toolbar slides down from top of list with count + action buttons. Esc deselects all.
interaction_details: Actions can have keyboard shortcuts shown.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Sticky at top, not floating. 2) Esc to clear. 3) Shortcuts displayed inline.

title: Inline collaborator hover-card with status
design_brief: Building user hover-card (think Github @mention hover, Linear). Pain point: hover-cards are generic.
ux_reasoning: Hovering an @mention reveals card with avatar, name, role, status, local time. Click for full profile.
interaction_details: Card shows avatar, name, role, status, local time.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Local time shown — global team context. 2) 350ms intent delay. 3) Anchored to mention.

title: Number input with +/- buttons and click-hold acceleration
design_brief: Building stepper input (think quantity in cart, dimensions in design tool). Pain point: click each + over and over.
ux_reasoning: +/- buttons. Holding accelerates: starts at 1/200ms, after 500ms speeds to 1/50ms, after 1000ms speeds to 1/20ms.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Hold-to-accelerate — Apple keyboard backlight pattern. 2) Three speed tiers. 3) Direct input still works.

title: Bubble chart with size + color + position encoding
design_brief: Building 3-dim viz (think NYT GDP-by-country, Gapminder). Pain point: bubble charts often illegible at small sizes.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Bubbles too small or too large
ux_reasoning: 3 dimensions: X position, Y position, bubble area (not diameter — area matches perception). Color = 4th dim if needed. Hover bubble: details.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Area-not-diameter encoding (perceptually correct). 2) 3-4 dimensions in one chart. 3) Tooltip per bubble.

title: Migrate-from-other-tool wizard inline in settings
design_brief: Building data import (think Notion import from Evernote). Pain point: import is its own onboarding journey.
ux_reasoning: Import wizard lives as a card in settings → Import. Source selector, file upload, mapping. User can interrupt and resume.
interaction_details: Each step rendered fully, with next button.
accessibility_considerations: Real semantic. Progress indicator with aria-valuenow.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Inline, not modal — interruption-safe. 2) Progress bar at top. 3) Each step is fully its own page within the card.
